title: Max Number of K-Sum Pairs
description:
You are given an integer array nums and an integer k.

In one operation, you can pick two numbers from the array whose sum equals k and remove them from the array.

Return _the maximum number of operations you can perform on the array_.

solutionCode.python:
```python
def findLengthOfShortestSubarray(arr):
    n = len(arr)
    left, right = 0, n - 1

    while left < n - 1 and arr[left] <= arr[left + 1]:
        left += 1

    if left == n - 1:
        return 0

    while right > 0 and arr[right] >= arr[right - 1]:
        right -= 1

    result = min(right, n - left - 1)

    i, j = 0, right

    while i <= left and j < n:
        if arr[i] <= arr[j]:
            result = min(result, j - i - 1)
            i += 1
        else:
            j += 1

    return result
```

    
    The algorithm starts by finding the longest non-decreasing prefixes and suffixes of the input array. After finding the left and right boundaries, we check if the entire array is non-decreasing (i.e., `left == n - 1`). If so, we can return 0 as no subarray needs to be removed.

Next, we initialize the result as the minimum of the length of the right boundary and the complement of the left boundary, ensuring that we cover the case where the removal occurs at either end.

We then iterate through the array with two pointers `i` and `j`, starting with `i=0` and `j=right`, and compare the elements at these indices. If `arr[i] <= arr[j]`, we update the result by taking the minimum of the distance between `i` and `j` minus 1 or the current result, and increment `i`. Otherwise, we increment `j`. The loop runs until both pointers have traversed the full array.

The function finally returns the minimum length subarray that needs to be removed to make the remaining elements non-decreasing.
